front: What is Terraform and why do Data Engineers use it?
back:
Terraform is an open-source Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tool by HashiCorp that lets you define, provision, and manage cloud resources using declarative configuration files.

Why DEs use it:
- Provision data infrastructure (S3, RDS, Redshift, EMR) reproducibly
- Version-control your infrastructure alongside your data pipelines
- Spin up identical dev/staging/prod environments
- Tear down expensive clusters when not in use (cost control)
- Onboard new team members fast: one 'terraform apply' sets up the whole stack

Core workflow:
1. Write HCL config files
2. terraform init (download providers)
3. terraform plan (preview changes)
4. terraform apply (create/update resources)
5. terraform destroy (tear down when done)

Terraform appears in ~40% of senior DE job postings. It is the de-facto standard for cloud infrastructure provisioning.
